product_overview: A React + Material UI frontend for managing employee records (login, list, add, edit, view, delete) with search/filtering and theme toggling. The app communicates with a backend API to persist employee data and uses routes /login, /list, and /form for primary flows.
core_goals: Allow users to authenticate and access the employee management interface.
Provide full CRUD (create, read, update, delete) operations for employees with responsive UI and dialogs.
Enable fast client-side search and filtering across employee name, email, and position.
Support a consistent navigation bar and theme toggle (light/dark) for the entire app.
Ensure clear user feedback for loading, success, and error states.

features.name: User Login
features.description: Authenticate a user with username and password to gain access to the application; successful login redirects to the employee list.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill username input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Login button to submit -> Redirected to /list
Navigate to /login -> Fill username input field -> Fill password input field (invalid) -> Click Login button to submit -> See error message on invalid credentials

features.name: Employee List
features.description: Display all employees in a searchable, filterable table including loading and empty-result states.
user_flows: Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> See loading message while data is being fetched -> See employees listed in a table with ID, Name, Email, Position columns
Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Type in the search field to filter employees by name, email, or position -> See filtered results in the table
Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Type in the search field to filter employees by name, email, or position -> See "No employees found." when no results match

features.name: Add Employee
features.description: Create a new employee either via the add dialog from the list page or via the standalone add form page; show success and error feedback and refresh the list on success.
user_flows: Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "+ Add Employee" button to open the add dialog -> Fill Name input field -> Fill Email input field -> Fill Position input field -> Click "Add Employee" button to submit the form -> See success alert on successful addition -> Dialog closes and list refreshes after successful add

Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /form -> Fill Name input field -> Fill Email input field -> Fill Position input field -> Click "Add Employee" button to submit the form -> See success alert on successful addition -> Navigate to /list or return to list where new employee appears

Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "+ Add Employee" button to open the add dialog -> Fill Name input field -> Fill Email input field with invalid format -> Fill Position input field -> Click "Add Employee" button to submit the form -> See error alert on failure

features.name: View Employee
features.description: Inspect a single employee's details in a dialog opened from the employee list.
user_flows: Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "View" button on an employee row -> See employee details (ID, Name, Email, Position) in a dialog -> Click "Close" button to dismiss the dialog
Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "View" button on an employee row -> See error message if employee details cannot be loaded

features.name: Edit Employee
features.description: Edit an existing employee's information using a pre-filled form dialog; support update, cancel, success feedback, and refresh.
user_flows: Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "Edit" button on an employee row -> See form pre-filled with employee data in a dialog -> Modify Name, Email, or Position fields -> Click "Update Employee" button to submit changes -> See success alert on successful update -> Dialog closes and list refreshes after successful edit

Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "Edit" button on an employee row -> See form pre-filled with employee data in a dialog -> Modify fields -> Click "Cancel" to close without saving -> See dialog dismissed and list unchanged

Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "Edit" button on an employee row -> Modify Email to invalid format -> Click "Update Employee" button to submit changes -> See error alert on failure

features.name: Delete Employee
features.description: Remove an employee from the system via a confirmation dialog; provide confirmation, cancelation, success feedback, and list refresh.
user_flows: Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "Delete" button on an employee row -> See confirmation dialog asking "Are you sure you want to delete employee <name>?" -> Click "Delete" button to confirm deletion -> See success notification after successful deletion -> List refreshes after deletion

Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "Delete" button on an employee row -> See confirmation dialog asking "Are you sure you want to delete employee <name>?" -> Click "Cancel" to cancel deletion -> See employee remain in the list
Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> Click "Delete" button on an employee row -> Click "Delete" button to confirm deletion -> See error notification on failure

features.name: Navigation Menu
features.description: Top app bar that provides primary navigation links, logoff, and theme toggle; shows links conditionally based on authentication.
user_flows: Be authenticated (valid session) -> Navigate to /list -> See "Employee Manager" title in the app bar -> Click "Add Employee" link to navigate to /form -> Click "Employee List" link to navigate to /list -> Click "Logoff" button to log out and redirect to /login
Be unauthenticated -> Navigate to /login -> See only "Login" link in the menu bar

features.name: Theme Toggle
features.description: Allow users to switch the UI between light and dark themes using an icon in the menu bar.
user_flows: Be on any page with the menu bar (e.g., /list) -> Click the sun/moon icon button in the menu bar -> See the UI switch between light and dark themes
Be on any page with the menu bar -> Click the sun/moon icon button in the menu bar -> UI fails to update -> See no visual change and display an optional error state
